expectations.text: Output contains all 6 rounds (Round 1 through Round 6)
expectations.evidence: All 6 rounds present: 'Round 1: Architect Proposes', 'Round 2: Operator Challenges', 'Round 3: Architect Defends', 'Round 4: Operator Stress-Tests', 'Round 5: Architect Final Stand', 'Round 6: CTO Decides'

expectations.text: Architect, Operator, and CTO personas all appear and are distinct
expectations.evidence: Architect appears in Rounds 1, 3, 5; Operator appears in Rounds 2, 4; CTO appears in Round 6. Each has a distinct role: Architect proposes/defends, Operator challenges/stress-tests, CTO decides.

expectations.text: Every challenge in Round 2 has a severity rating (critical/significant/minor)
Round 2 challenges are categorized (reliability, complexity, migration-risk, team-capability, cost, dependency, security)
Round 4 references specific mitigations from Round 3, not just repeating Round 2 challenges
expectations.evidence: Stress-test 1 attacks the adapter becoming a 'provider-agnostic auth interface' (Round 3 defense). Stress-test 2 attacks the ticket categorization gate (Round 3 concession). Stress-test 3 attacks Auth0 Actions as claim compatibility layer (Round 3 defense). Stress-test 4 attacks the circuit breaker fallback (Round 3 mitigation).

expectations.text: Round 6 contains one of the 5 valid verdicts: proceed, proceed-with-conditions, prototype-first, defer, reject
expectations.evidence: Verdict is 'proceed-with-conditions' stated explicitly in Round 6.

expectations.text: Metrics table present with all 8 metrics filled in (challengesRaised, stressTestsRaised, challengesSurvived, challengesConceded, mitigationsHeld, mitigationsBroken, proposalModified, confidenceScore)
expectations.evidence: Debate Metrics table contains all 8 metrics: challengesRaised=7, stressTestsRaised=4, challengesSurvived=2, challengesConceded=3, mitigationsHeld=2, mitigationsBroken=2, proposalModified=yes, confidenceScore=7

expectations.text: Concrete action items with who/what/when structure
expectations.evidence: 7 action items each with assignee (e.g., 'Auth-domain engineer 1'), task description, and deadline date (e.g., 'by 2026-04-05').

expectations.text: Round 5 either modifies the original proposal or explicitly justifies keeping it unchanged
expectations.evidence: Round 5 ends with 'Proposal modifications after two rounds of challenge' listing 5 specific modifications including temporary adapter, pre-migration categorization, limited Auth0 Actions scope, revised outage handling, and extended timeline.

expectations.text: Round 5 includes fallback/escape-hatch plans for surviving challenges
expectations.evidence: Each of the 4 items in Round 5 includes explicit 'Fallback plan:' sections, e.g., 'if the adapter proves too complex during Phase 1, abort and fall back to the hardening approach' and 'if categorization shows fewer than 50% backend auth bugs, we pivot to the hardening approach.'
